seo_description: Current time in Quito, Ecuador. Live ECT clock — UTC-5 year-round, no Daylight Saving Time. Quito time vs New York, London, Miami, Bogotá.

faq.question: What time is it in Quito right now?
faq.answer: Quito, the capital of Ecuador, operates on Ecuador Time (ECT/UTC-5) year-round. Ecuador has never observed Daylight Saving Time, making ECT a permanent, fixed offset. At UTC-5, Quito shares its offset with Eastern Standard Time (EST) in the US — meaning Quito and New York are on the same time when New York is in winter (EST). Use the live clock above for the exact current time.

faq.question: What time zone is Quito in?
faq.answer: Quito is in the Ecuador Time zone (ECT), fixed at UTC-5 year-round. This is the same offset as US Eastern Standard Time (EST). Because Ecuador does not observe DST, the relationship between Quito and New York changes seasonally: they are on the same time during US winter (EST), but Quito is 1 hour behind New York when New York switches to Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) in summer.

faq.question: Does Quito observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: No. Ecuador has never observed Daylight Saving Time. Quito maintains UTC-5 every day of the year. This makes scheduling with Quito straightforward — there are no bi-annual clock changes to account for. However, because the US, Europe, and other trading partners do adjust their clocks, the offset between Quito and those cities varies by one hour depending on the season.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Quito and New York?
faq.answer: Quito and New York are on the same time when New York observes Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5), typically from early November to mid-March. When New York switches to Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) from mid-March to early November, Quito becomes 1 hour behind New York. When it is 12:00 PM noon in New York (EDT) in summer, it is 11:00 AM in Quito.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Quito and London?
faq.answer: Quito is 5 hours behind London when London is on GMT (UTC+0) in winter, and 6 hours behind when London switches to BST (UTC+1) in summer. When it is 3:00 PM in London (GMT), it is 10:00 AM in Quito. London-based businesses should call Quito in the afternoon to catch morning working hours in Ecuador.

faq.question: What is special about Quito's geographic position?
faq.answer: Quito sits almost exactly on the equator (0°13'S latitude), making it one of the world's few capital cities located directly on this line. The Mitad del Mundo monument north of the city marks the equatorial line. Despite being in the tropics, Quito's high altitude of approximately 2,850 meters (9,350 feet) gives it a mild spring-like climate year-round. The equatorial location means equal day and night lengths throughout the year — roughly 12 hours of daylight every day.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Quito?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Quito are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM ECT on weekdays, with a 1–2 hour lunch break typically from 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM or 3:00 PM. Government offices generally operate 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Banks are open 9:00 AM to 4:30 PM Monday through Friday, and some branches open Saturday from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM. Shopping centers like El Jardín and Quicentro operate 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM daily.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Quito from the United States?
faq.answer: From the US East Coast (EST in winter), Quito shares the same UTC-5 offset — any time during US business hours also falls within Quito's working day. In summer (EDT/UTC-4), Quito is 1 hour behind, so call by 6:00 PM Eastern to reach a 5:00 PM Quito closing time. From the West Coast (PST/UTC-8), Quito is 3 hours ahead — call from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM Pacific to reach Quito during its core business hours.

content_blocks.title: Quito Time Zone Guide — Ecuador Time (ECT, UTC-5)
content_blocks.content: Quito, the capital of Ecuador, uses Ecuador Time (ECT), permanently fixed at UTC-5 with no Daylight Saving Time. Situated at 0°13'S, 78°30'W on the western slopes of the Andes at an elevation of approximately 2,850 meters, Quito holds the distinction of being one of the closest capital cities to the equator and one of the highest. The UTC-5 offset places Quito in the same timezone as US Eastern Standard Time (EST) in winter — when New York is also on EST, they read the same clock. In summer, when the US East Coast shifts to EDT (UTC-4), Quito falls one hour behind. Colombia and Peru, Ecuador's neighbors, use UTC-5 (COT) and UTC-5 (PET) respectively with no DST, giving the entire central Andean region a consistent UTC-5 anchor. Quito is 5–6 hours behind London, 10–11 hours behind Paris and Berlin, and 10–11 hours behind Madrid. The IANA timezone identifier is America/Guayaquil (shared with continental Ecuador; the Galápagos Islands use a separate UTC-6 offset).

content_blocks.title: Quito as a Gateway City — Timezone and Trade
content_blocks.content: Quito serves as a major hub for South American commerce, with Mariscal Sucre International Airport connecting the city to North America, Europe, and the wider region. Ecuador's dollarized economy (the US dollar has been the official currency since 2000) creates a naturally close financial relationship with the United States. The shared UTC-5 winter offset means New York financial markets open at 9:30 AM Eastern — exactly 9:30 AM in Quito during winter months, eliminating any need for conversion during that period. The Galápagos Islands, Ecuador's world-famous archipelago and UNESCO World Heritage Site located in the Pacific Ocean, operate on a separate UTC-6 timezone — one hour behind mainland Quito. For travelers and businesses, this means internal scheduling between Quito and the Galápagos requires a one-hour adjustment. Ecuador's growing oil, banana, shrimp, and cut flower export industries connect the country closely to US and EU markets, with business typically conducted during the overlapping morning-to-midday window that suits both regions.

content_blocks.title: Calling Quito Internationally — Best Windows by Region
content_blocks.content: Ecuador Time (ECT/UTC-5) is among the simpler Latin American offsets for North American callers. From New York during winter (EST/UTC-5), Quito is on the same clock — any call between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM Eastern lands squarely within Quito business hours. During summer (EDT/UTC-4), New York is 1 hour ahead: calling before 6:00 PM Eastern ensures you reach Quito offices before 5:00 PM local. From Miami (same timezone as New York), the same windows apply. From Los Angeles (PST/UTC-8 in winter), Quito is 3 hours ahead — call 6:00 AM to 2:00 PM Pacific to cover the full 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Quito day. From London (GMT/UTC+0), Quito is 5 hours behind — afternoon calls from 2:00 PM to 10:00 PM GMT reach Quito's 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM window. From Madrid (CET/UTC+1 in winter), calling 3:00 PM to 11:00 PM local time covers Quito's business hours. From Dubai (GST/UTC+4), reaching Quito requires calling from around midnight to early morning, making asynchronous communication more practical. Ecuador's country code is +593, and Quito's area code is 2.
